steps.title: Identify the action's outcome
steps.content: The key clue is “so high that Gogol must tip his head back in order to see”—this reveals the effect of the action described by raise.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
The word "raise" needs to mean "move upward" — the father sends the kite so high Gogol must tip his head back. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — Strengthen: "his father strengthen a kite into the wind" — strengthen means to make more powerful, not to move something upward. ✗
  • Choice B — Lift: "his father lift a kite into the wind" — lift means to move upward, matching the kite climbing so high it becomes a distant speck. ✓
  • Choice C — Celebrate: "his father celebrate a kite into the wind" — celebrate means to mark an occasion, unrelated to the physical action described. ✗
  • Choice D — Group: "his father group a kite into the wind" — group means to collect things together, which makes no sense for flying a kite. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
